steps.title: Find the base area
steps.content: Total surface area equals base area plus lateral surface area. Subtract: $$48,672 = \text{base} + 27,936$$ $$\text{base} = 20,736 \text{ square inches}$$

steps.title: Find the base side length
steps.content: The base is a square with area 20,736. For a square: $$s^2 = 20,736$$ $$s = 144 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Find the slant height
steps.content: For a square pyramid, lateral surface area equals $2sl$, where $l$ is the slant height: $$27,936 = 2(144)l$$ $$27,936 = 288l$$ $$l = 97 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Apply Pythagorean theorem to find pyramid height
steps.content:
In a right square pyramid, the slant height $l$, height $h$, and distance from center to edge midpoint form a right triangle.

This distance is $s/2 = 72$: $$l^2 = h^2 + 72^2$$ $$97^2 = h^2 + 72^2$$ $$9,409 = h^2 + 5,184$$ $$h^2 = 4,225$$ $$h = 65 \text{ inches}$$ That matches Choice A — $65$.
